title: Needle in the log
prompt: grep searches INSIDE files: grep WORD FILE prints every line containing WORD. server.log has 121 lines and exactly one mentions the word magic — that line carries the flag. Find it with grep, save the whole line into answer.txt (grep's output can go straight to a file with >), and submit the flag.

checks.pattern: ACCESS GRANTED: the magic word is

details.restate: Two steps: get the one line of server.log that contains the word magic into a file called answer.txt, and type the flag from that line into the flag box.

io.because: grep filters a file down to the lines that contain your word

io.returns: (nothing on screen — the output went into the file)
io.because: > redirects output into a file instead of the terminal

help.plain: grep pulls the one interesting line out of 121; > saves what grep found into answer.txt.
help.analogy: A metal detector on a beach — you don't sift every grain of sand, you walk the detector and it beeps at the one thing that matters.
help.mnemonic: grep WORD FILE, then bolt on  > answer.txt  to keep it.
help.nudge: You already have the command that PRINTS the line. What one symbol sends printed output into a file instead?
